task_prompt: A researcher proposes using a random forest classifier trained on 500 synthetic samples generated from a known Gaussian mixture model. The model is evaluated using 5-fold cross-validation on the same synthetic distribution. The researcher claims 95% accuracy proves the approach generalizes to real-world data with similar but unknown distribution parameters.

A team fine-tunes a pre-trained language model on 10,000 customer support tickets to classify intent. They use stratified train/test splits and report macro-F1 of 0.82. However, the training data contains temporal patterns — newer tickets use different terminology. The team acknowledges this but argues the model captures semantic intent rather than surface patterns. Whether temporal shift degrades performance is an empirical question requiring a time-stratified evaluation.

A researcher implements a standard logistic regression pipeline with L2 regularization for binary classification of tabular medical data. They use nested cross-validation (5-fold outer, 3-fold inner for hyperparameter tuning), report AUC-ROC with confidence intervals via bootstrap, and compare against a random baseline and a decision tree. The regularization strength is selected from {0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0} using the inner CV loop. All preprocessing (standardization, missing value imputation) is fit on training folds only.
